name: warm-day-setback-heat-cool-mode
description: Warm day + thermostat in heat_cool mode: dual setpoints (setback_heat/setback_cool) applied instead of HVAC off (Issue #96)
notes: Issue #96: On a warm day the classifier sets hvac_mode='off', but when the thermostat
is currently running in heat_cool (auto) mode the correct behavior is to apply dual
This widens the comfort band for the day without forcing a full HVAC-off command,
allowing the thermostat to stay in heat_cool mode and only engage when indoor temp
drifts outside the widened setback band.
The indoor temp (71F) is above comfort_heat (70F) so the warm-day comfort-gap guard
does NOT fire — the system proceeds directly to dual setback.
Expected outcome at classification time: dual_setback_applied with
HVAC mode must remain 'heat_cool' — set_hvac_mode('off') must NOT be called.
The Decision.target_temp field carries target_temp_low for dual-setpoint decisions;
a future simulator extension may add target_temp_high.
Simulator support: the simulator does not model heat_cool mode at all currently.
Issue #96 adds heat_cool awareness in both the simulator and automation.py.
All assertions are marked simulator_support=false until that is implemented.

events.note: Morning temps: indoor 71F (above comfort_heat 70F), outdoor 62F on a warm day
Classifier issues warm-day hvac_mode=off. Thermostat is in heat_cool (auto) mode. Expected: dual setpoints low=60F high=82F, no mode change.

assertions.reason: warm day + thermostat in heat_cool mode — dual setback applied: target_temp_low=setback_heat (60F), target_temp_high=setback_cool (82F); HVAC mode unchanged
